content.problemStatement:
Chef gave you an array AAA of length NNN which contains pairwise **distinct** elements. You can perform the following operations on the array:


Select two distinct indices i,ji,ji,j and swap Ai,AjA_i,A_jAi​,Aj​
He then asked you to find the **minimum** number of operations to sort the array.


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of every test case contains an integer NNN - the length of array.
The second line of every test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,..,AN A_1,A_2,..,A_NA1​,A2​,..,AN​ denoting the integers in the array.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line- the answer to the iii-th test case.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 5*10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N distinct integers A1, A2, ..., AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of swaps required to sort the array in non‑decreasing order.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: swap positions 1 and 2.
Test case 2: swap (1,4) then (2,4).
Test case 3: three swaps are needed.
Test case 4: already sorted, zero swaps.
The permutation consists of a 2‑cycle (positions 1 and 3) and a fixed point; only one swap is required.
Cycles: (1 4), (2 3), (5 6) each of length 2 → 1 swap per cycle, total 3 swaps.

solution.approach: The array can be viewed as a permutation that maps each current index to the index where its element should be in the sorted order. Decomposing this permutation into cycles gives the answer: a cycle of length L can be sorted with L‑1 swaps. Therefore the minimum number of swaps equals N minus the number of cycles. The algorithm: 1) copy the array with original indices, 2) sort the copy to know the target position of each element, 3) build an array pos where pos[i] is the index where element at i should go, 4) run a DFS/while loop over unvisited indices to count cycle lengths and accumulate swaps.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case for sorting, plus O(N) for cycle detection. Overall O( Σ N log N ).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for auxiliary arrays (sorted copy, position mapping, visited flags).
